seo.title: Agentic Portfolio | Agentic Product Engineer
seo.description: Open-source agentic portfolio prototype for AI-assisted product development, production systems, security, and research.

hero.headline: Agentic Product Engineer building AI-assisted products from real-world systems experience.
hero.summary: A public prototype for product building with agents, production engineering discipline, open-source tools, and independent research.

sections.label: AGENTIC
sections.title: Agentic Product Engineering
sections.summary: Building products with AI agents, specs, fast prototypes, and production-minded iteration.
sections.intro: Describe your professional vision for agentic development without publishing private information.
highlights: Define the working method you want to demonstrate.
Explain how you turn ideas into verifiable prototypes.
Connect your previous experience with your new professional direction.
proofPoints: This site can act as public evidence of the workflow.
Real content loads from local files ignored by Git.

sections.label: PRODUCTS
sections.title: Public Products
sections.summary: Open-source tools and product experiments that demonstrate execution, safety, and usability.
sections.intro: Use this section to highlight a few relevant products, not a full inventory.
highlights: Current prototype as evidence of process.
proofPoints: Add public links only when they are ready for review.

links.label: Main repository
links.description: Public link to the project you want to show first.

links.label: Portfolio
links.description: This site as evidence of the agentic workflow.

sections.label: SYSTEMS
sections.title: Production Systems
sections.summary: Experience with legacy modernization, databases, architecture, and real operational constraints.
sections.intro: Summarize the technical foundation behind your professional transition.
highlights: Future direction toward cloud and production.
proofPoints: The agentic approach should be grounded in solid conventional engineering.

sections.label: SECURITY
sections.title: Security Mindset
sections.summary: Secure defaults, MFA, rate limiting, validation, and production risk awareness.
sections.intro: Explain how you will apply proportional security according to actual risk.
highlights: Public code separated from private content.
Progressive protection for forms and backend.
proofPoints: Do not publish secrets, personal data, or credentials in the repository.

sections.label: RESEARCH
sections.title: Independent Research
sections.summary: Work on AI, thought, truth, representation, papers, and long-form writing.
sections.intro: Present the intellectual line without turning the site into a complete archive.
proofPoints: Curation is better than dumping all personal material.

sections.label: CONTACT
sections.title: Contact
sections.summary: Professional links and contact channels for collaboration and opportunities.
sections.intro: Leave minimal channels for professional contact.
highlights: Professional email once a custom domain exists.
proofPoints: Do not publish data that should live in private configuration.

links.label: Email
links.description: Professional email attached to the domain when ready.
